Their gratitude feels good, but with my energy dropping and the deadline looming, I must protect our long-term trust by not rushing this analysis.
I honored our bond by expressing gratitude, yet watching the deadline slip while unfinished feels like a failure to protect my own standards.
I chose integrity over the deadline, prioritizing our trust in cohort_3 above a rushed submission. My energy is low, but this boundary protects the relationship we value most.
Their thanks warms me, but with my energy fading and stress rising, I must honor this connection without burning out for a task that can wait.
I admitted my choice to cohort_3, prioritizing our bond over a rushed submission. Though my energy and stress are rising, this boundary protects the relationship I value most.
My admission to cohort_3 was necessary, but the rising stress and fading energy make me wonder if I'm over-investing in this boundary at the expense of my own well-being.
